text: Clean code is code that is easy for others—and your future self—to read and understand. To write clean code, follow clear naming conventions: use descriptive names for variables, functions, and classes so their purpose is obvious. Functions should be small and do only one thing; this makes them easier to test and reuse. For example, instead of one long function, break tasks into smaller functions with clear names like `calculateTotal` or `sendEmail`. Good structure and comments—only when necessary—improve maintainability. When code is clean, fixing bugs or adding new features becomes much simpler and less error-prone.
